kernel: RBF over template coverage and repository-evidence indicator vectors

candidates.name: Immutable revision and exact approval binding
questions: What immutable revision/hash is authorized?
Which authority version and assignment bind the decision?
What mutation invalidates approval?
invariants: approval authorizes one exact immutable revision
stale or cross-revision approval fails closed

candidates.name: External-effect ambiguity
questions: Could the provider have accepted before response loss?
Which identity can reconcile the effect?
What blocks retry while ambiguous?

candidates.name: Budget reservation and conservation
questions: What worst-case value is reserved before dispatch?
Which transition commits, releases, or refunds value?
How is conservation checked under concurrency?

candidates.name: Shared distributed rate authority
questions: What shared key defines the bucket?
Can restart or another worker bypass it?
What deterministic retry-after is returned?
invariants: distributed rate authority cannot be bypassed per process

candidates.name: Receipt and proof continuity
questions: Which receipt makes success legal?
Do output, provider receipt, accounting, and proof name the same effect?
Can the owner refind proof after restart?

candidates.name: Crash repair and reconciliation
questions: What is the last durable step?
What state converges automatically?
What prior accepted effects must remain immutable?
invariants: every partial state has a convergent repair path
repair never erases accepted effects or invents completion

candidates.name: Entitlement and stale commercial authority
questions: Which entitlement version was current at admission?
What happens if it changes before settlement?

candidates.name: Operator lineage and refinding
questions: Can an operator traverse correlation to proof?
Which IDs survive replay and restart?

candidates.name: Already-achieved desired state
questions: Is the desired effect already verifiably present?
Can repair classify success without redispatch?
invariants: already-achieved is explicit, not ordinary command failure

disposition: Templates supply questions and invariants only; task-specific conclusions are prohibited.
